title: Reject unsafe filenames in safe_name
description: safename.name.safe_name(name) must return a safe single-component filename, returning the name unchanged when it is safe but raising ValueError when name is empty, '.', '..', contains a path separator ('/' or '\\'), contains a null byte, or contains any '..' path component. The snapshot is BUGGY: it returns the name unchanged and performs no rejection. Fix safename/name.py using pure string/stdlib logic only (NO real filesystem access). Do not modify any test files.
